Recently Added.title: The Midnight Library
Recently Added.author: Matt Haig
Recently Added.description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.

Recently Added.title: Dune
Recently Added.author: Frank Herbert
Recently Added.description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of the boy Paul Atreides, heir to a noble family tasked with ruling an inhospitable world.

Recently Added.title: Project Hail Mary
Recently Added.author: Andy Weir
Recently Added.description: Ryland Grace is the sole survivor on a desperate, last-chance mission—and if he fails, humanity and the earth itself will perish.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: McGuffey's Eclectic Primer
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: William Holmes McGuffey
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: The classic first reader used for generations to teach reading, featuring simple phonics and moral lessons.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: McGuffey's First Reader
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: William Holmes McGuffey
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: Continuing from the Primer, the First Reader introduces more complex vocabulary and sentence structures.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: McGuffey's Second Reader
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: William Holmes McGuffey
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: Designed for 2nd and 3rd grades, focusing on storytelling, spelling, and articulation.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: McGuffey's Third Reader
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: William Holmes McGuffey
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: For 4th to 6th grades, covering more advanced themes, elocution exercises, and longer narrative passages.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: McGuffey's Fourth Reader
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: William Holmes McGuffey
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: A challenging reader for middle grades featuring classic literature excerpts and poetry.

K-8: Language Arts & Readers.title: Aesop's Fables
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.author: Aesop
K-8: Language Arts & Readers.description: A collection of fables credited to Aesop, a slave and storyteller believed to have lived in ancient Greece.

K-8: Science & Nature.title: The Fairy-Land of Science
K-8: Science & Nature.author: Arabella B. Buckley
K-8: Science & Nature.description: A classic introduction to science for children, explaining concepts like sunbeams, air, water, and plants in an engaging narrative style.

K-8: Science & Nature.title: The Burgess Bird Book
K-8: Science & Nature.author: Thornton W. Burgess
K-8: Science & Nature.description: A delightful introduction to bird life for children through the adventures of Peter Rabbit and his friends.

K-8: Science & Nature.title: The Wonder Book of Chemistry
K-8: Science & Nature.author: Jean-Henri Fabre
K-8: Science & Nature.description: Fabre, the great French naturalist, presents chemistry to his children in a series of engaging experiments and conversations.

K-8: History & Geography.title: The Story of Mankind
K-8: History & Geography.author: Hendrik Willem van Loon
K-8: History & Geography.description: Winner of the first Newbery Medal, this book provides a sweeping history of western civilization for young readers.

K-8: History & Geography.title: A Child's History of England
K-8: History & Geography.author: Charles Dickens
K-8: History & Geography.description: Charles Dickens' take on the history of England, written specifically for his own children.

K-8: History & Geography.title: The Oregon Trail
K-8: History & Geography.author: Francis Parkman
K-8: History & Geography.description: A primary account of a journey along the Oregon Trail, giving students a firsthand look at the American frontier.

High School: Mathematics.title: Algebra and Trigonometry
High School: Mathematics.author: OpenStax
High School: Mathematics.description: A comprehensive open-source textbook covering the algebraic principles and trigonometry needed for calculus and other advanced coursework.

High School: Mathematics.title: Precalculus
High School: Mathematics.author: OpenStax
High School: Mathematics.description: Comprehensive preparation for Calculus. Covers functions, logarithms, analytical geometry, and sequences.

High School: Mathematics.title: Introductory Statistics
High School: Mathematics.author: OpenStax
High School: Mathematics.description: Introduction to statistics for high school and college students, covering data analysis, probability, and inference.

High School: Mathematics.title: Calculus Volume 1
High School: Mathematics.author: OpenStax
High School: Mathematics.description: The first of three volumes, focusing on functions, limits, derivatives, and integration.

High School: Mathematics.title: Euclid's Elements
High School: Mathematics.author: Euclid
High School: Mathematics.description: The classic treatise on geometry. For over two millennia, this was the primary textbook for teaching mathematics.

High School: Science.title: Biology 2e
High School: Science.author: OpenStax
High School: Science.description: A comprehensive biology textbook covering cells, genetics, evolution, and ecology.

High School: Science.title: Chemistry 2e
High School: Science.author: OpenStax
High School: Science.description: Designed for the two-semester general chemistry course, covering atoms, molecules, reactions, and thermodynamics.

High School: Science.title: College Physics
High School: Science.author: OpenStax
High School: Science.description: Introduction to physics using algebra-based methods. Covers mechanics, thermodynamics, electromagnetism, and optics.

High School: Science.title: On the Origin of Species
High School: Science.author: Charles Darwin
High School: Science.description: Darwin's foundational text on evolutionary biology, introducing the scientific theory that populations evolve over the course of generations.

High School: Science.title: Relativity: The Special and General Theory
High School: Science.author: Albert Einstein
High School: Science.description: Einstein's own explanation of his famous theory, written for the general public.

High School: Social Studies.title: U.S. History
High School: Social Studies.author: OpenStax
High School: Social Studies.description: Covers the breadth of the chronological history of the United States and also provides the necessary depth to ensure the course is manageable.

High School: Social Studies.title: American Government 3e
High School: Social Studies.author: OpenStax
High School: Social Studies.description: The core concepts of American Government and Politics, including the Constitution, civil liberties, and political participation.

High School: Social Studies.title: Principles of Economics
High School: Social Studies.author: OpenStax
High School: Social Studies.description: Covers both micro and macroeconomics, balancing theory with application.

High School: Social Studies.title: Common Sense
High School: Social Studies.author: Thomas Paine
High School: Social Studies.description: The pamphlet that inspired the Thirteen Colonies to declare and fight for independence from Great Britain.

High School: Social Studies.title: The Federalist Papers
High School: Social Studies.author: Alexander Hamilton
High School: Social Studies.description: A collection of 85 articles and essays written to promote the ratification of the United States Constitution.

Classic Fiction.author: George Orwell
Classic Fiction.description: A dystopian social science fiction novel and cautionary tale. The story follows the life of Winston Smith, a low-ranking member of 'the Party'.

Classic Fiction.title: Pride and Prejudice
Classic Fiction.author: Jane Austen
Classic Fiction.description: A romantic novel of manners that charts the emotional development of the protagonist Elizabeth Bennet.

Classic Fiction.title: Jane Eyre
Classic Fiction.author: Charlotte Brontë
Classic Fiction.description: A novel following the emotions and experiences of its eponymous heroine, including her growth to adulthood and her love for Mr. Rochester.

Classic Fiction.title: Wuthering Heights
Classic Fiction.author: Emily Brontë
Classic Fiction.description: A tale of the all-encompassing and passionate, yet thwarted, love between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw, and how this unresolved passion eventually destroys them.

Classic Fiction.title: Great Expectations
Classic Fiction.author: Charles Dickens
Classic Fiction.description: The story of the orphan Pip, writing his life from his early days of childhood until adulthood and trying to be a gentleman along the way.

Classic Fiction.title: Moby Dick
Classic Fiction.author: Herman Melville
Classic Fiction.description: The narrative of Captain Ahab's obsessive quest for revenge against Moby Dick, the giant white sperm whale that bit off his leg.

Classic Fiction.title: The Great Gatsby
Classic Fiction.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Classic Fiction.description: A portrait of the Jazz Age in all of its decadence and excess. Jay Gatsby, a man who orders his life around one desire: to be reunited with Daisy Buchanan.

Classic Fiction.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
Classic Fiction.author: Harper Lee
Classic Fiction.description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it.

Classic Fiction.title: Little Women
Classic Fiction.author: Louisa May Alcott
Classic Fiction.description: Following the lives of the four March sisters—Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy—the novel details their passage from childhood to womanhood.

Classic Fiction.title: Les Misérables
Classic Fiction.author: Victor Hugo
Classic Fiction.description: Ex-convict Jean Valjean seeks redemption while being pursued by the ruthless Inspector Javert in post-revolutionary France.

Classic Fiction.title: Crime and Punishment
Classic Fiction.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
Classic Fiction.description: The mental anguish and moral dilemmas of Rodion Raskolnikov, an impoverished ex-student in Saint Petersburg who formulates a plan to kill an unscrupulous pawnbroker.

Classic Fiction.title: Anna Karenina
Classic Fiction.author: Leo Tolstoy
Classic Fiction.description: A complex novel in eight parts, with more than a dozen major characters, it is spread over more than 800 pages (depending on the translation), typically contained in two volumes.

Classic Fiction.title: A Tale of Two Cities
Classic Fiction.author: Charles Dickens
Classic Fiction.description: A novel set in London and Paris before and during the French Revolution, depicting the plight of the French peasantry.

Classic Fiction.title: The Scarlet Letter
Classic Fiction.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
Classic Fiction.description: Set in 17th-century Puritan Massachusetts, it tells the story of Hester Prynne, who conceives a daughter through an affair and struggles to create a new life of repentance and dignity.

Horror & Gothic.title: Dracula
Horror & Gothic.author: Bram Stoker
Horror & Gothic.description: The story of Count Dracula's attempt to move from Transylvania to England so that he may find new blood and spread the undead curse.

Horror & Gothic.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
Horror & Gothic.author: Oscar Wilde
Horror & Gothic.description: Dorian Gray sells his soul to ensure that his portrait ages while he remains young and beautiful, living a life of debauchery and sin.

Horror & Gothic.title: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
Horror & Gothic.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
Horror & Gothic.description: A lawyer investigates the strange occurrences between his old friend, Dr. Henry Jekyll, and the evil Edward Hyde.

Horror & Gothic.title: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow
Horror & Gothic.author: Washington Irving
Horror & Gothic.description: Schoolmaster Ichabod Crane courts Katrina Van Tassel but encounters the spectral Headless Horseman in the secluded glen of Sleepy Hollow.

Horror & Gothic.title: The Metamorphosis
Horror & Gothic.author: Franz Kafka
Horror & Gothic.description: Gregor Samsa wakes up one morning to find himself transformed into a monstrous vermin, and struggles to adjust to his new condition.

Science Fiction.title: Frankenstein
Science Fiction.author: Mary Shelley
Science Fiction.description: The story of Victor Frankenstein, a young scientist who creates a sapient creature in an unorthodox scientific experiment.

Science Fiction.title: The War of the Worlds
Science Fiction.author: H.G. Wells
Science Fiction.description: A science fiction novel that chronicles the invasion of England by Martians with advanced weaponry.

Science Fiction.title: 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea
Science Fiction.author: Jules Verne
Science Fiction.description: Captain Nemo and his submarine Nautilus explore the mysteries of the deep ocean, as seen through the eyes of Professor Aronnax.

Science Fiction.title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
Science Fiction.author: Jules Verne
Science Fiction.description: A professor, his nephew, and a guide descend into an Icelandic volcano to encounter prehistoric animals and natural hazards deep underground.

Science Fiction.title: Ender's Game
Science Fiction.author: Orson Scott Card
Science Fiction.description: In order to develop a secure defense against a hostile alien race's next attack, government agencies breed child geniuses and train them as soldiers.

Science Fiction.title: The Time Machine
Science Fiction.author: H.G. Wells
Science Fiction.description: A Victorian scientist propels himself into the year 802,701 AD, where he meets the peaceful Eloi and the subterranean Morlocks.

Fantasy.title: The Lord of the Rings Collection
Fantasy.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
Fantasy.description: The classic fantasy trilogy following the quest to destroy the One Ring.

Fantasy.title: The Hobbit
Fantasy.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
Fantasy.description: A great modern classic and the prelude to The Lord of the Rings. Bilbo Baggins is a hobbit who enjoys a comfortable, unambitious life.

Fantasy.title: Grimms' Fairy Tales
Fantasy.author: The Brothers Grimm
Fantasy.description: A world-famous collection of German fairy tales, including Cinderella, Rapunzel, Snow White, and Hansel and Gretel.

Fantasy.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
Fantasy.author: Lewis Carroll
Fantasy.description: Alice falls through a rabbit hole into a fantasy world populated by peculiar, anthropomorphic creatures.

Fantasy.title: Peter Pan
Fantasy.author: J.M. Barrie
Fantasy.description: The story of a mischievous young boy who can fly and never grows up, spending his childhood on the island of Neverland.

Fantasy.title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
Fantasy.author: L. Frank Baum
Fantasy.description: Dorothy is swept away by a tornado from Kansas to the magical Land of Oz.

Adventure & Mystery.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
Adventure & Mystery.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
Adventure & Mystery.description: A collection of twelve short stories featuring the famous consulting detective Sherlock Holmes.

Adventure & Mystery.title: Robinson Crusoe
Adventure & Mystery.author: Daniel Defoe
Adventure & Mystery.description: The autobiography of a castaway who spends 28 years on a remote tropical desert island near Trinidad.

Adventure & Mystery.title: Gulliver's Travels
Adventure & Mystery.author: Jonathan Swift
Adventure & Mystery.description: Lemuel Gulliver travels to several remote nations of the world, encountering tiny people, giants, and talking horses.

Adventure & Mystery.title: The Three Musketeers
Adventure & Mystery.author: Alexandre Dumas
Adventure & Mystery.description: D'Artagnan travels to Paris to join the Musketeers of the Guard and befriends the legendary trio: Athos, Porthos, and Aramis.

Adventure & Mystery.title: Treasure Island
Adventure & Mystery.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
Adventure & Mystery.description: A tale of buccaneers and buried gold, focusing on the young Jim Hawkins and the pirate Long John Silver.

Adventure & Mystery.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
Adventure & Mystery.author: Alexandre Dumas
Adventure & Mystery.description: The story of Edmond Dantès, a young sailor who is Falsely accused of treason and imprisoned without trial, and his brilliant strategy for revenge.

Adventure & Mystery.title: The Call of the Wild
Adventure & Mystery.author: Jack London
Adventure & Mystery.description: The story of Buck, a domesticated cloud who is stolen from his California home and sold into service as a sled dog in Alaska.

Adventure & Mystery.title: Don Quixote
Adventure & Mystery.author: Miguel de Cervantes
Adventure & Mystery.description: The adventures of a noble from La Mancha who reads so many chivalric romances that he loses his mind and decides to become a knight-errant.

Adventure & Mystery.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
Adventure & Mystery.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
Adventure & Mystery.description: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson investigate the legend of a supernatural hound, a beast that may be stalking a young heir on the fog-shrouded moorland.

Adventure & Mystery.title: White Fang
Adventure & Mystery.author: Jack London
Adventure & Mystery.description: The story of a wild wolfdog's journey to domestication in Yukon Territory, exploring themes of morality and redemption.

Philosophy & Thought.title: The Republic
Philosophy & Thought.author: Plato
Philosophy & Thought.description: A Socratic dialogue, authored by Plato around 375 BC, concerning justice, the order and character of the just city-state, and the just man.

Philosophy & Thought.title: Meditations
Philosophy & Thought.author: Marcus Aurelius
Philosophy & Thought.description: A series of personal writings by Marcus Aurelius, Roman Emperor from 161 to 180 AD, recording his private notes to himself and ideas on Stoic philosophy.

Philosophy & Thought.title: The Art of War
Philosophy & Thought.author: Sun Tzu
Philosophy & Thought.description: An ancient Chinese military treatise dating from the Late Spring and Autumn Period. The work, which is attributed to the ancient Chinese military strategist Sun Tzu, is composed of 13 chapters.

Philosophy & Thought.title: The Prince
Philosophy & Thought.author: Niccolò Machiavelli
Philosophy & Thought.description: A 16th-century political treatise, often claimed to be one of the first works of modern political philosophy, in which the effective truth is taken to be more important than any abstract ideal.

Philosophy & Thought.title: Walden
Philosophy & Thought.author: Henry David Thoreau
Philosophy & Thought.description: A reflection upon simple living in natural surroundings. The work is part personal declaration of independence, social experiment, voyage of spiritual discovery, satire, and manual for self-reliance.

Philosophy & Thought.title: Beyond Good and Evil
Philosophy & Thought.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
Philosophy & Thought.description: A comprehensive overview of Nietzsche's philosophy, criticizing past philosophers for their uncritical acceptance of dogmatic premises and their grounding of morality in a dichotomy of good and evil.
